title: BrainSaw agar stock solution
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"82b92","text":"Making agar stock solution for serial sectioning imaging.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"d34rk","text":"Hot agar can be painful but is not dangerous. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: You will need at least 100 mL of 50 mM PB. BrainSaw 50 mM pH 7.4 PB slicing buffer by Rob Campbell
Rinse out the 100 ml bottle in which the stock is kept.
Fill the bottle with 100 mL of 50 mM PB to the notched fill line.
Weigh out 5 g of Agarose multipurpose, molecular biology grade MP Biomedicals Catalog #11AGAP0100-CF and pour into the bottle. Cap the bottle and shake it.

Do not heat the stock solution bottle. This concentrates it over time and it takes ages to heat. Instead, pour out the desired amount into a beaker and heat carefully in the microwave. Use low or medium power and stop as it begins to boil or even just before. The agar will look slightly less clear if you don't boil it, but this does not matter. As a rule of thumb: melt just over 10 ml for a single brain. Just under 20 ml for 4 brains.
creator: Rob Campbell
